content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/utils/outside-hook.ts

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 17
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-select' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu

Code context:
```
   16            target instanceof Element &&
   17            target.closest('.pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu');
   18  

```

### Fix 2
Line: 17
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select__menu' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-select__menu' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu

Code context:
```
   16            target instanceof Element &&
   17            target.closest('.pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu');
   18  

```

### Fix 3
Line: 17
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-select]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-select' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu

Code context:
```
   16            target instanceof Element &&
   17            target.closest('.pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu');
   18  

```

### Fix 4
Line: 17
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-select__menu' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-select__menu' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  matchingText: .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu

Code context:
```
   16            target instanceof Element &&
   17            target.closest('.pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v5-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu');
   18  

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/utils/outside-hook.ts
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me reason through all 4 fixes:

**Current state of line 17:**
```
target.closest('.pf-v6-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu, .pf-v6-c-menu, .pf-v5-c-select__menu');
```

It looks like a previous pass already changed `.pf-v5-c-menu` → `.pf-v6-c-menu` (the first and third selectors), but the `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` references (second and fourth selectors) remain.

**Fix 1 & Fix 3**: Remove `.pf-v5-c-select` references. Looking at the selector, there's no bare `.pf-v5-c-select` — only `.pf-v5-c-select__menu`. But the original code (per the incident context) had `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` which contains the `pf-v5-c-select` block. The `.pf-v5-c-menu` was already migrated to `.pf-v6-c-menu`. These fixes target the class that no longer exists.

**Fix 2 & Fix 4**: Remove `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` references. This class was removed with no replacement.

**Analysis**: In PF v6, the Select component was rebuilt on top of the Menu component. So `pf-v5-c-select__menu` no longer exists — the correct class is just `pf-v6-c-menu` (which is already present). The purpose of this code is to detect clicks on PatternFly menus/dropdowns rendered via portals. In v6, all of these use `.pf-v6-c-menu`.

**Solution**: Remove the dead `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` selectors. The `.pf-v6-c-menu` is already present (and duplicated). Clean up to just `.pf-v6-c-menu` (deduplicated).
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. The behavior is preserved — the hook still detects clicks on PatternFly menus/dropdowns rendered via portals, using the valid v6 class `.pf-v6-c-menu`.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select` | **Applied** | The `.pf-v5-c-menu` references had already been migrated to `.pf-v6-c-menu` by a previous pass. The remaining dead selectors (`.pf-v5-c-select__menu`) and duplicates were cleaned up. The selector now uses only `.pf-v6-c-menu`. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Removed both `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` references. In PF v6, Select is rebuilt on Menu, so `.pf-v6-c-menu` is the correct replacement (already present). |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select` | **Applied** | Same line as Fix 1 — the `pf-v5-c-select`-based classes were removed; `.pf-v6-c-menu` covers the use case. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Same line as Fix 2 — both `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` instances removed. |

**Additional cleanup**: Deduplicated the `.pf-v6-c-menu` selector (it appeared twice in the original due to the duplicated selector list pattern). The final selector is simply `'.pf-v6-c-menu'`.

| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Removed both `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` references. In PF v6, Select is rebuilt on Menu, so `.pf-v6-c-menu` is the correct replacement (already present). |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select` | **Applied** | Same line as Fix 1 — the `pf-v5-c-select`-based classes were removed; `.pf-v6-c-menu` covers the use case. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-select-menu` | **Applied** | Same line as Fix 2 — both `.pf-v5-c-select__menu` instances removed. |
